creator: rscheff@freebsd.org
text:
If I see the actual work being done correctly, then each instance of the writer is sending, over the loopback interface, data starting at 4k up to 1M, in 1k incremenets

(there is a problem btw - the writer starts at the syspage (line 195), the reader has 4k hardcoded (line 134), btw; as the reader only exists once a sufficent amount of data has been transferred, it may either stop prematurely or wait excessively for data which never arrives) also, the test seems to fall apart on IPv6 only systems (or when localhost doesn't resolve to an IPv4).

My box doesn't have any packet losses at all on lo0 - the SACK code would only get excersized when there is packet loss happening (which would be rather unexpected on a loopback interface).

Can you repro the issue while "tcpdump -i lo0" (or whatever interface the name you are using resolves to) is running?

If yes, then maybe the crash contains sufficient data to piece together what exactly triggered this on a (probably) lossy loopback device.
